2008-01-01.title: In Reversal, Pakistan Welcomes Outside Help With  Inquiry on Bhutto
2008-01-01.abstract: Pakistan’s ambassador to the U.S. said his government would not endorse a separate inquiry modeled after one carried out by the U.N. after the assassination of Rafik Hariri of Lebanon in 2005.

2008-01-01.title: Fighting Intensifies After Election in Kenya
2008-01-01.abstract: Kenya sank deeper into trouble, with a curfew imposed in Kisumu, the country’s third-largest city, ethnic fighting intensifying and more than 100 people killed in election-related violence.

2008-01-01.title: Israel: Olmert Curbs Settlements
2008-01-01.abstract: Prime Minister Ehud Olmert has sent a letter to defense, housing and agriculture ministers, saying that his and the defense minister’s authorization would be required for any new building, planning or land expropriation for Jewish settlements in the West Bank.

2008-01-01.title: Gay Muslims Pack a Dance Floor of Their Own
2008-01-01.abstract: The monthly club night known as Gayhane is an all-too-rare opportunity for gay Muslims to merge their immigrant cultures and their sexual identities.

2008-01-01.title: Iraqi Revelers Embrace the New Year
2008-01-01.abstract: But even as partygoers embraced the New Year, a surge of attacks on Monday served as a potent reminder that 2007 was the bloodiest on record.

2008-01-02.title: Crisis in Kenya
2008-01-02.abstract: The crisis over the disputed elections in Kenya worsened.

2008-01-02.title: Mob Sets Kenya Church on Fire, Killing Dozens
2008-01-02.abstract: After a disputed election, ethnic violence is threatening to engulf Kenya, formerly one of the most stable in Africa.

2008-01-02.title: U.S. Isn’t Ready to Accept Pakistan’s Initial Findings
2008-01-02.abstract: As Pakistan postponed its elections until February, American intelligence analysts questioned the evidence against a suspect in Benazir Bhutto’s assassination.

2008-01-02.title: Pakistan’s Days of Rage Leave Cloud of Uncertainty
2008-01-02.abstract: A gray area pervades everything in Karachi, from big questions about the country’s direction to the smallest details of life.

2008-01-02.title: Chávez’s Promised Hostage Release Fizzles, His Second Major Setback in Weeks
2008-01-02.abstract: The failed mission has exposed Venezuela’s president, Hugo Chávez, to criticism of misplaced priorities.

2008-01-03.title: Justice Dept. Sets Criminal Inquiry on C.I.A. Tapes
2008-01-03.abstract: An inquiry into the agency’s destruction of interrogation tapes was elevated to a formal criminal investigation.

2008-01-03.title: Unwilling New Frontier for Migrants: 3 Greek Isles
2008-01-03.abstract: Three Greek islands, Samos, Lesbos and Chios, have become new fault lines for immigration into the European Union.

2008-01-03.title: Egypt Permits Pilgrims to Cross Back Into Gaza
2008-01-03.abstract: Egypt allowed about 2,000 Palestinian pilgrims to cross back into the Gaza Strip on Wednesday after they had been stranded for days in the Egyptian Sinai on their way back from Mecca.

2008-01-03.title: Britain: Patients Flee Fire at Top Hospital
2008-01-03.abstract: A fire blazed across the top floors of the Royal Marsden Hospital in London, one of Europe’s leading cancer centers.

2008-01-03.title: Pakistani Opposition Parties Decry Election Delay
2008-01-03.abstract: The main opposition parties denounced the government’s decision on Wednesday to postpone parliamentary elections but said they would abide by the ruling.

2008-01-04.title: Kenyan Opposition Rally Blocked
2008-01-04.abstract: In Nairobi, Kenya's capital, riot police pushed back thousands of opposition supporters who poured into the streets to answer a call for a million-person rally that had been banned by the government.

2008-01-04.title: Chaos in Kenya
2008-01-04.abstract: Chaos and ethnic fighting has ignited in Kenya, destabilizing the country, just one week after Kenyans went to the polls to elect their president.

2008-01-04.title: An Inquiry Seen as Payback in a Rivalry
2008-01-04.abstract: The Justice Department’s criminal inquiry into the destruction of the Central Intelligence Agency interrogation tapes will be carried out largely by agents from the Federal Bureau of Investigation.

2008-01-04.title: Shiite Praises Anti-Insurgent Militias
2008-01-04.abstract: In a speech in Najaf, the Shiite holy city, Abdul Aziz al-Hakim, said a major reason for recent security improvements was in part due to a reliance on tribal groups and local councils.

2008-01-04.title: Mexico: President’s Cousin Briefly Abducted
2008-01-04.abstract: A cousin of President Felipe Calderón was abducted at gunpoint, beaten and held for four hours on Wednesday in Morelia, in Michoacán State, newspapers reported.

2008-01-05.title: A Respite From Violence
2008-01-05.abstract: Despite continuing political tension between Kenya's president and  opposition leaders, life was edging back to normal in many parts.

2008-01-05.title: A Respite From Violence
2008-01-05.abstract: Despite continuing political tension between Kenya's president and  opposition leaders, life was edging back to normal in many parts.

2008-01-05.title: Anti-Abortion Bill Imperils Italy’s Governing Coalition
2008-01-05.abstract: The Vatican is backing an Italian opposition proposal to make it harder to end pregnancies, threatening to open new divisions in Prime Minister Romano Prodi’s coalition.

2008-01-05.title: Proposal to Better Unite City Leaves Dresden Divided
2008-01-05.abstract: Opponents of the proposed four-lane, 2,100-foot-long span Waldschlösschen Bridge say it will mar the famous vistas of Dresden, Germany, and the picturesque river that divides it.

2008-01-05.title: North Korea Says Earlier Disclosure Was Enough
2008-01-05.abstract: North Korea rejected the United States’ demand for a more thorough account on whether it has tried to enrich uranium for atomic weapons.

2008-01-06.title: Kenyan City Is Gripped by Violence
2008-01-06.abstract: Few places have been so thoroughly gutted by the post-election violence in Kenya as Kisumu,  the country’s third-largest city.

2008-01-06.title: 1977 Exam Opened Escape Route Into China’s Elite
2008-01-06.abstract: For a whole generation consigned to the countryside, a university entrance examination three decades ago was a chance to escape what seemed like a life sentence of tedium and hardship.

2008-01-06.title: Killing in Nicaragua Makes Spectacle of the Courts
2008-01-06.abstract: The conviction of an American citizen on charges of rape and murder, and his subsequent release, has put Nicaragua’s justice system on trial.

2008-01-06.title: Israeli Army Operation in Nablus Is Suspended
2008-01-06.abstract: The large-scale, three-day operation drew strong condemnation from Palestinian officials, who argue that it could frustrate peace talks and destabilize the restive city.

2008-01-06.title: Exit Polling Suggests Election Victory for Georgia’s Pro-Western President
2008-01-06.abstract: Voters in Georgia went to the polls in an early presidential election called by President Mikheil Saakashvili after he declared a state of emergency in November to deal with protesters.

2008-01-07.title: Congo Opens Talks on Ending Fighting in Eastern Region
2008-01-07.abstract: Talks to secure peace in conflict-torn eastern Congo opened amid warnings by the Roman Catholic Church against a “Balkanization” of the vast African country.

2008-01-07.title: Sri Lankans Kill 36 Rebels as Monitors Begin Pullout
2008-01-07.abstract: Tamil Tiger rebels said they lost one of their leaders in heavy fighting in northern Sri Lanka, as Norwegian-led monitors began pulling out before the formal end of a tattered truce.

2008-01-07.title: Poland Signals Doubts About Planned U.S. Missile-Defense Bases on Its Territory
2008-01-07.abstract: Saying that it feels no threat from Iran, Poland’s foreign minister says his country’s new government is not ready to accept American missile-defense bases until all costs and risks are considered.

2008-01-07.title: Bomber Kills 4 on Iraqi Army Day
2008-01-07.abstract: As jubilant Iraqi soldiers celebrated Army Day by dancing and chanting anti-insurgent slogans inside a downtown office building here on Sunday, a suicide bomber attempted to shove his way through the gate.

2008-01-07.title: Israel Says It Will Toughen Response to Longer-Range Fire
2008-01-07.abstract: Israeli security officials said that a rocket with a longer range than usual fired by Palestinian militants from Gaza on Thursday had been made in Iran.

2008-01-08.title: U.S. Describes Confrontation With Iranian Boats
2008-01-08.abstract: Five Iranian boats took aggressive actions around three U.S. naval vessels in the Strait of Hormuz, an official said.

2008-01-08.title: Canada: Appeal in Serial Killer Case
2008-01-08.abstract: Prosecutors in British Columbia asked an appeals court to set aside six second-degree murder convictions against Robert Pickton, and order a new trial on 26 charges of first-degree murder. Relatives and friends of many of the victims were disappointed that Mr. Pickton, a pig farmer arrested in 2002, was found guilty by a jury last month only of second-degree murder, which does not require proof of premeditation. The prosecutors said the judge made errors, including his decision to split the case and put Mr. Pickton on trial on 6 counts of first-degree murder last year, with a trial on the remaining 20 counts to be held this year. Even if the government appeal is successful and Mr. Pickton is convicted on all 26 counts of first-degree murder, it will not change his sentence of life in prison without the chance of parole for 25 years. The remains of the victims, mostly prostitutes, were found buried on his farm in a Vancouver suburb.

2008-01-08.title: Suicide Bomber Kills Key Sunni Leader
2008-01-08.abstract: At least 14 people were killed in the latest attack on nationalist Sunnis who have allied themselves with American troops.

2008-01-08.title: Israeli Troops Kill 3 Armed Palestinians
2008-01-08.abstract: Israeli soldiers shot dead three armed Palestinians in Gaza and the West Bank, including a woman, an army spokeswoman said. The armed woman, 23, identified by Islamic Jihad as one of its members, and a man approached the border fence near the Erez Crossing in northern Gaza at noon, the spokeswoman said, and opened fire on soldiers nearby before being shot. In a village near the West Bank city of Jenin, soldiers fired at armed men who approached a checkpoint, the army spokeswoman said, killing one. Palestinian officials identified the dead man as a member of Islamic Jihad.

2008-01-08.title: A Politically Dangerous Liaison for France’s President
2008-01-08.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy’s romance with a singer has fueled criticism that he is ignoring the country.

2008-01-09.title: At Gaza’s Edge, Israelis Fear Rockets’ Whine
2008-01-09.abstract: Sderot, Israel, a working-class town less than two miles from Gaza, has been repeatedly hit with rockets.

2008-01-09.title: Jean-Claude Vrinat, Owner of Famed Paris Restaurant, Is Dead at 71
2008-01-09.abstract: For more than three decades, Mr. Vrinat was the owner of the Taillevent restaurant in Paris, which is regarded by many as the pinnacle of elegance in French cuisine.

2008-01-09.title: Mideast Leaders Agree to Core Talks
2008-01-09.abstract: The Israeli and Palestinian leaders authorized the start of negotiations on the sensitive core issues of the conflict.

2008-01-09.title: Britain: Bhutto’s Son Critical of U.S.
2008-01-09.abstract: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, the 19-year-old son of Benazir Bhutto, Pakistan’s assassinated opposition leader, said at a news conference in London that the United States would have to stop “supporting dictators” in Pakistan for Islamic extremism to be defeated there. Returning to Britain from Pakistan two weeks after his mother’s death to resume his first-year studies at Oxford, he gave a battery of feisty answers to reporters who questioned the Pakistan Peoples Party’s decision to pick him as his mother’s successor as party chairman. To a reporter who suggested he had been appointed to the post “like a piece of family furniture,” he said he had not sought it, but added, “It was recognized at this moment in crisis the party needed a close association with my mother through the bloodline.” Asked if he feared for his life, he replied: “How many Bhuttos can you kill? From every house a Bhutto will come.” Noting that the party had selected his father, Asif Ali Zardari, as the party’s co-chairman until he finishes his university degree, he said he would step “gradually and carefully” into politics. He renewed the family’s demands for a United Nations inquiry into his mother’s death and spoke out against American support for the generals who have ruled Pakistan for more than half its 60-year existence. “The problem is that dictatorships breed extremism, and once the U.S. stops supporting dictators, we can successfully tackle extremists,” he said.

2008-01-09.title: Suharto Suffers More Setbacks
2008-01-09.abstract: Doctors struggled to stabilize the health of the former president Suharto after a setback in which there were new signs of internal bleeding and fluid in his lungs.

2008-01-10.title: Fear and Anger in Kenya
2008-01-10.abstract: Kenya's recent election has untapped decades of resentment,  displacing thousands of people and claiming around 500 lives.

2008-01-10.title: Consultant Questions Beijing’s Claim of Cleaner Air
2008-01-10.abstract: The new study found flaws in Beijing’s “Blue Sky” system of air-quality monitoring and noted that the city changed its formula for measuring pollution in 2006, using less polluted locales than before.

2008-01-10.title: Colombia: Plan for New Hostage Mission
2008-01-10.abstract: President Hugo Chávez of Venezuela said Colombian guerrillas had provided him with the location of two captives, Clara Rojas, a former aspirant to Colombia’s vice presidency, and Consuelo González, a former Colombian lawmaker.

2008-01-10.title: Ex-C.I.A. Aide Won’t Testify on Tapes Without Immunity
2008-01-10.abstract: Jose A. Rodriguez Jr., who in 2005 ordered the destruction of videotapes of harsh interrogations, will not offer testimony that might subsequently be used against him.

2008-01-10.title: Richardson Drops Out of Democratic Race
2008-01-10.abstract: It is unclear whether Gov. Bill Richardson’s supporters will gravitate to Sen. Barack Obama or Sen. Hillary Clinton.

2008-01-11.title: Remembering Sir Edmund Hillary
2008-01-11.abstract: Sir Edmund Hillary, who won worldwide acclaim in 1953 by becoming the first to scale the summit of Mount Everest, has died.

2008-01-11.title: Many Palestinians Offer a Bleak Opinion of Bush
2008-01-11.abstract: As President Bush arrived in the region, many ordinary Palestinians say his support for Israel is at their expense.

2008-01-11.title: Iran Shows Its Own Video of Vessels’ Encounter in Gulf
2008-01-11.abstract: The new video, broadcast by Iran’s English-language satellite channel, Press TV, showed a member of Iran’s Revolutionary Guard sending a radio message to the American vessels.

2008-01-11.title: North Korea: U.S. Suggests a New Nuclear Deadline
2008-01-11.abstract: Christopher R. Hill, the State Department’s point man on North Korea, urged it to give a full accounting of its nuclear weapons programs before the new president of South Korea takes office.

2008-01-11.title: Annan to Help in Kenya, Group Says
2008-01-11.abstract: The African Union announced that Kofi Annan, the former secretary general of the United Nations, will try to broker a truce in Kenya.

2008-01-12.title: China: Horse Racing to Return
2008-01-12.abstract: The government has approved regular horse racing at the Orient Lucky City racecourse in Wuhan, the main city in Hubei Province, starting in September, and is considering allowing legal gambling on the races next year on a trial basis, the official Xinhua news agency reported.

2008-01-12.title: Bush, Leaving Israel, Keeps Up Push for Peace Treaty
2008-01-12.abstract: President Bush promised to return in May for Israel’s 60th anniversary to press for a peace treaty between Israel and the Palestinians by the end of his term.

2008-01-12.title: A Clergyman of the Streets Leaves His Historic Pulpit
2008-01-12.abstract: The Rev. Christian Führer will have to step down as leader of his historic Protestant church in Leipzig, Germany, because he is turning 65, the church’s mandatory retirement age.

2008-01-12.title: U.N. Nuclear Official Urges Iran to Clarify ‘Outstanding Issues’
2008-01-12.abstract: Mohamed ElBaradei, the chief of the International Atomic Energy Agency, said that Iran needed to make its nuclear activities more transparent.

2008-01-12.title: Food From Cloned Animals Seems Safe, a Panel Finds
2008-01-12.abstract: Meat and milk from cloned animals seem to pose no special health risks, said a report released by the European Food Safety Agency. It was a first step toward the eventual sale of such products within the European Union.

2008-01-13.title: Bush Urges Arab Allies to Confront Iran
2008-01-13.abstract: A speech by the president criticizing Iran came as Tehran agreed to answer remaining questions about its nuclear programs.

2008-01-13.title: French Cabinet Position Not Enough? Then Try Mayor.
2008-01-13.abstract: The races for France’s municipal elections in March have not started, but nearly two-thirds of the 33 members of the president’s cabinet have transformed themselves into part-time politicians.

2008-01-13.title: Bush Says He Could Halt Withdrawal, if Security Dictates
2008-01-13.abstract: A year after he ordered a large increase in troops in Iraq, the president said that he was prepared to slow or halt further reductions.

2008-01-13.title: Afghan Police Struggle to Work a Beat in a War
2008-01-13.abstract: Undermined by corruption and poor discipline, Afghanistan’s police force remains weak, though its expected role is daunting.

2008-01-13.title: Opposition Wins Taiwan Parliamentary Election
2008-01-13.abstract: The opposition Kuomintang party won a landslide victory as President Chen Shui-bian resigned as chairman of the governing Democratic Progressive Party to take responsibility for the loss.

2008-01-14.title: Taiwan Election May Ease Tensions With China
2008-01-14.abstract: Victory for the opposition could sharply reduce the political influence of President Chen Shui-bian, who has antagonized China and frustrated the United States.

2008-01-14.title: Ex-Baathists Get a Break. Or Do They?
2008-01-14.abstract: A measure to open jobs to former members of Saddam Hussein’s party is riddled with loopholes and caveats.

2008-01-14.title: An Iraqi House Was Rigged to Kill American Soldiers
2008-01-14.abstract: House bombs are a common threat in Diyala, where American troops have found five since the start of last week’s operation against Al Qaeda in Mesopotamia.

2008-01-14.title: Woman Released by Guerrillas Returns to Colombia to See Son
2008-01-14.abstract: Freed by Colombian guerrillas after six years of captivity in jungle camps, Clara Rojas, a former aspirant to the vice presidency of Colombia, traveled to Bogotá to be reunited with her 3-year-old son.

2008-01-14.title: Error by Allies and 2 Clashes Kill 15 in South of Afghanistan
2008-01-14.abstract: The Netherlands Defense Ministry said Dutch troops had killed two of their own in Oruzgan Province in southern Afghanistan and two allied Afghan soldiers that they mistook for enemies, according to The Associated Press.

2008-01-15.title: U.S. Army Chief in Europe to Run NATO Afghan Unit
2008-01-15.abstract: Gen. David D. McKiernan, who oversaw the allied ground attack that toppled Saddam Hussein in 2003, is expected to be appointed as the next commander of NATO forces in Afghanistan.

2008-01-15.title: A Favorite Meal, Now Offering a Side Order of Environmental Awareness
2008-01-15.abstract: A new London restaurant has worked hard to offer fish that is legally caught and not overexploited, but it does not come cheap.

2008-01-15.title: Rice Makes Unannounced Visit to Baghdad
2008-01-15.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice flew to Baghdad, hoping to build momentum behind reconciliation efforts.

2008-01-15.title: Olmert Is Cautious as Talks With Palestinians Begin
2008-01-15.abstract: As the talks started, Prime Minister Ehud Olmert of Israel sought to lower expectations of reaching a final peace agreement within a year.

2008-01-15.title: U.S. Plans Sale of 900 Missiles to Saudi Arabia
2008-01-15.abstract: The $123 million deal is considered a mostly symbolic gesture, meant to bolster military relations with Saudi Arabia and other Persian Gulf states.

2008-01-16.title: The Week Ahead
2008-01-16.abstract: Alison Smale, of the International Herald Tribune, discusses events in the world this week.

2008-01-16.title: Visiting American Admiral Confers with Chinese Generals
2008-01-16.abstract: The four-day visit by Admiral Timothy Keating, head of the U.S. Pacific Command, followed a diplomatic spat last year.

2008-01-16.title: ‘The Kite Runner’ Film Outlawed in Afghanistan
2008-01-16.abstract: The Culture Ministry has quietly banned the import of the film based on the best-selling novel about childhood betrayal, ethnic tension and sexual predation in Afghanistan.

2008-01-16.title: Spate of Police Commander Murders in Mexico
2008-01-16.abstract: Gunmen assassinated three police commanders, along with the wife and two daughters of one of them, in Tijuana. The killings came after police arrested 11 people on organized-crime charges.

2008-01-16.title: Pope Cancels Speech After Protest at University
2008-01-16.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI has canceled a speech at the prestigious La Sapienza University in Rome amid opposition by professors and students who say he is hostile to science.

2008-01-17.title: Dispute Deepens for Russia and Britain
2008-01-17.abstract: A British cultural organization was forced to close its offices and its staff was questioned by Russian authorities, the latest twist in a downward spiral in relations since the death of a former Russian spy in London in 2006.

2008-01-17.title: French Court Finds Oil Company Partly Liable for Spill
2008-01-17.abstract: In the first ruling of its kind for responsibility in maritime pollution, the oil company Total was fined almost $300 million in damages.

2008-01-17.title: Egypt’s Problem and Its Challenge: Bread Corrupts
2008-01-17.abstract: Subsidized bread is part of making ends meet in Egypt, where about 45 percent of the population survives on just $2 a day.

2008-01-17.title: Pakistan Fort Overrun by Militants
2008-01-17.abstract: Hundreds of Islamic militants attacked a paramilitary fort in the restive South Waziristan tribal region, killing 22 soldiers and taking several others hostage.

2008-01-17.title: Sarkozy Uses French Clout as His Own Slips
2008-01-17.abstract: The French president signed deals in the United Arab Emirates to create a French military base there, as part of a strategy to project power in the region.

2008-01-18.title: Peshawar Under Siege
2008-01-18.abstract: Situated near the eastern end of the Khyber Pass, Peshawar has long been a gateway to Afghanistan. But in the past year, Pakistan's war with the Taliban and Al Qaeda has spilled right into the Pakistani frontier city, killing policemen and soldiers and scaring its famously tough citizens.

2008-01-18.title: 11 Die in Attack on a Shiite Mosque in Iraq
2008-01-18.abstract: Witnesses said at least 11 people were killed in a blast outside a Shiite mosque in restive Diyala Province.

2008-01-18.title: 12 Killed in Suicide Bombing at Shiite Mosque in Pakistan
2008-01-18.abstract: Twelve people were killed and 25 others were wounded, three of them seriously, when a suicide bomber detonated explosives on Thursday in a Shiite Muslim mosque in Peshawar.

2008-01-18.title: Rockets Hit Israel, Whose Strikes Kill 5 Palestinians
2008-01-18.abstract: In reaction to the latest surge of violence, which started after an Israeli military operation on Tuesday, the Israeli defense minister ordered the closing of the border with Gaza.

2008-01-18.title: Gates Seeks Troop Estimates
2008-01-18.abstract: Defense and military officials have been asked to prepare projections so that President Bush can balance troop requests for Iraq against other needs.

2008-01-19.title: C.I.A. Sees Qaeda Link in the Death of Bhutto
2008-01-19.abstract: The C.I.A. has concluded that the assassins who killed Benazir Bhutto were directed by Baitullah Mehsud, a Pakistani militant leader.

2008-01-19.title: 2 Belgian Tourists Killed in Yemen Ambush
2008-01-19.abstract: The attack, the first aimed at foreigners since last summer, took place in an area that is not considered especially dangerous.

2008-01-19.title: Germany: Another Cub With Fans
2008-01-19.abstract: A female polar bear cub born this month at Nuremberg Zoo and being hand-raised has officially been named Flocke, or Snowflake.

2008-01-19.title: Head Scarf Debate Intensifies in Turkey
2008-01-19.abstract: The country’s secular elite vehemently opposes any loosening of the restriction on the Islamic head scarf, which is strictly forbidden in public buildings in Turkey.

2008-01-19.title: U.S. Sees Stalling by North Korea on Nuclear Pact
2008-01-19.abstract: The White House is split on how long to exercise patience without jeopardizing the fulfillment of an agreement.

2008-01-20.title: Congo Groups Blame Each Other for Killings
2008-01-20.abstract: Rebels and militias in eastern Congo blamed each other on Saturday for the deaths of as many as 40 civilians in fighting over the past two days.

2008-01-20.title: Thumbs Race as Japan’s Best Sellers Go Cellular
2008-01-20.abstract: In Japan, cellphone novels have not only infiltrated the mainstream but have come to dominate it.

2008-01-20.title: Radical Left, Iran’s Last Legal Dissidents, Until Now
2008-01-20.abstract: Political protest has been suppressed under Iran’s current government, especially if linked to the West. But the radical left, despite its antireligious message, has been permitted relative freedom.

2008-01-20.title: Intervention, Hailed as a Concept, Is Shunned in Practice
2008-01-20.abstract: Three years after the United Nations adopted a groundbreaking resolution to help it intervene to stop genocide, it has not helped the organization end the violence in Darfur.

2008-01-20.title: Caution: Elephants Brake for Food on Bangkok’s Roads
2008-01-20.abstract: Of all the illegal activities that animate the streets of Bangkok —  pirated DVDs and fake watches, brothels that call themselves saunas — roaming elephants stand out more than others.

2008-01-21.title: Gaza Power Plant Shuts Down Over Dispute
2008-01-21.abstract: Israel’s closing of its border crossings with Gaza remained in effect Sunday, and Gaza’s only electricity plant shut down because of a shortage of imported fuel needed to run it.

2008-01-21.title: Israel Is Set to Promote the Use of Electric Cars
2008-01-21.abstract: Tiny and bereft of oil, Israel will announce its support for a broad effort to promote the use of electric cars.

2008-01-21.title: Bomber Kills Sunni Allies of the U.S.
2008-01-21.abstract: A teenager holding a box of candy detonated an explosive belt, killing four people and wounding nine in the second major bomb attack to strike American allies in Anbar Province in two days.

2008-01-21.title: Doctors Say Suharto Shows Signs of Recovery
2008-01-21.abstract: In what they called an amazing recovery, doctors in Jakarta said Sunday that former President Suharto was beginning to move his hands and speak.

2008-01-21.title: Suharto, From Verge of Death, Is Making a Recovery
2008-01-21.abstract: In what they called an amazing recovery, doctors in Jakarta said that former President Suharto had begun to move his hands and speak and that he is set to begin physical therapy.

2008-01-22.title: Zambia: Nationwide Blackouts
2008-01-22.abstract: Zambia was plunged into darkness in what appeared to be the second nationwide power failure in three days. The country relies on hydroelectricity, and national blackouts are rare.

2008-01-22.title: Mexico Hits Drug Gangs With Full Fury of War
2008-01-22.abstract: Forces are not only hunting cartel leaders, but also going after their crews of gunslingers, who terrorize towns.

2008-01-22.title: As Alps Warm, a Snow-Deprived Ski Resort Sells for $1
2008-01-22.abstract: As global warming brings less snow, lower-altitude stations must rely more on costly snow machines as Swiss ski resorts struggle with new economic hardships.

2008-01-22.title: Israel Allows Some Supplies Into Gaza
2008-01-22.abstract: Aid officials warned that Gaza, gripped by fuel and electrical shortages, was a few days away from a health and food crisis.

2008-01-22.title: Iran Leader Backs Parliament in a Dispute With Ahmadinejad
2008-01-22.abstract: The supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, intervened after Iran’s president had refused to carry out a measure that required his government to supply gas to remote villages.

2008-01-23.title: Italy’s Premier Seeks to Bolster Support
2008-01-23.abstract: Italian Prime Minister Romano Prodi, who holds a one-vote majority in Parliament’s upper house, vowed to preserve his government ahead of a confidence vote on Wednesday.

2008-01-23.title: Pakistan: U.S. Commander and Army Chief Hold Talks
2008-01-23.abstract: Adm. William J. Fallon, the senior American military commander in the Middle East, arrived for a one-day visit and called on President Pervez Musharraf’s successor as army chief.

2008-01-23.title: Agreement on Proposal for New Iran Sanctions
2008-01-23.abstract: The content of the new United Nations Security Council resolution draft to induce Tehran to give up its nuclear program has not been announced.

2008-01-23.title: Turkmens End Holiday for Birthday of Ex-Leader
2008-01-23.abstract: The new president of Turkmenistan has made attempts to unhinge himself and the country from some of the most bizarre legacies of the former leader’s leadership, such as the flamboyant celebrations that were devoted to him.

2008-01-23.title: Mexico: Army Seizes Guns From Police
2008-01-23.abstract: The federal authorities stepped up their campaign against armed drug gangs and police corruption, sending soldiers to shut down temporarily a number of local police departments.

2008-01-24.title: Continued Violence in Kenya
2008-01-24.abstract: East Africa bureau chief Jeffrey Gettleman reports on continuing violence in Kenya.

2008-01-24.title: American-Backed Sunni Militias
2008-01-24.abstract: Attacks on American-backed predominantly Sunni militias threaten a fragile linchpin of the American strategy to pacify Iraq.

2008-01-24.title: Continued Violence in Kenya
2008-01-24.abstract: East Africa bureau chief Jeffrey Gettleman reports on continuing violence in Kenya.

2008-01-24.title: At Amateur Bullfights in Colombia, Alcohol and Blood Flow Freely
2008-01-24.abstract: Participants can seem like figures in a Hieronymus Bosch painting: intestines peeking out of bellies, bones protruding from fractured shins, blood spurting from gashes in the flesh.

2008-01-24.title: U.S. Envoy Wants Political Pact in Kenya
2008-01-24.abstract: The American ambassador to Kenya said that his worry about the post-election crisis was the possible rift among the country’s opposing politicians.

2008-01-25.title: Palestinians Hurry to Finish Their Shopping in Egypt
2008-01-25.abstract: RAFAH, Egypt  There was less joy than frenzy on Thursday at this border crossing, as Gazans and Gaza businessmen hurried to stock up while they still could.

2008-01-25.title: Swedes Ponder Whether Killer Can Be a Doctor
2008-01-25.abstract: A prestigious medical school was tipped off that one of its students had been a Nazi sympathizer, released on parole after being convicted in 2000 of a murder.

2008-01-25.title: 10 Die in Mistaken Afghan Firefight
2008-01-25.abstract: The victims, nine Afghan police officers and a civilian, were mistaken for Taliban and shot by American forces.

2008-01-25.title: Turkey to Alter Speech Law
2008-01-25.abstract: The expected move to weaken the law is considered a key measure of the country’s democratic maturity.

2008-01-25.title: Japan: Rules for Police Interrogations
2008-01-25.abstract: The national police, shown to have used coercive interrogation practices to secure confessions, issued questioning guidelines for the first time.

2008-01-26.title: Draft of New Iran Sanctions Restricts Cargo and Travel
2008-01-26.abstract: The elements of this third measure of sanctions tighten and extend earlier ones but do not go significantly beyond them.

2008-01-26.title: Car Bomb Kills a Top Lebanese Terrorism Investigator
2008-01-26.abstract: The explosion killed an intelligence official in charge of investigating a series of recent attacks and assassinations.

2008-01-26.title: Colombia: Rice Lobbies for Trade Deal
2008-01-26.abstract: Secretary of State Condoleezza Rice wrapped up a two-day visit with a strong show of support for President Álvaro Uribe, a close ally of the Bush administration who is seeking a trade agreement with the United States.

2008-01-26.title: Venezuelan Pleads Guilty in Cover-Up of Suitcase of Cash
2008-01-26.abstract: The suitcase contained $800,000 in cash intended as a campaign contribution from the Venezuelan government to Argentina’s president, U.S. prosecutors say.

2008-01-26.title: Conviction in Poisoning of Indonesian Rights Activist
2008-01-26.abstract: The prosecution of a former airline pilot for poisoning a human rights advocate on a commercial flight to Amsterdam in 2004 had become a test of the independence of Indonesia’s judiciary.

2008-01-27.title: Pakistan's Talibanization
2008-01-27.abstract: The man who until recently was Pakistan's most senior law enforcement official has criticized his government's failure to respond to the spreading Taliban insurgency.

2008-01-27.title: Rights Group Accuses Myanmar of Holding More Dissidents
2008-01-27.abstract: Four months after a violent crackdown on pro-democracy demonstrators, the government of Myanmar has stepped up arrests of dissenters, breaking a promise to the United Nations.

2008-01-27.title: French Trader in Custody After $7 Billion in Losses
2008-01-27.abstract: Investigators were expected to question the trader in connection with a massive fraud at Société Générale.

2008-01-27.title: Tehran’s Mayor Speaks of Making Iran Less Isolated
2008-01-27.abstract: The annual economic gathering here not only attracts those with power in business and politics but also offers a springboard for those who wish to wield it.

2008-01-27.title: American Woman Is Kidnapped in Afghanistan
2008-01-27.abstract: The woman and her Afghan driver were kidnapped by gunmen in Kandahar on her way to work, the provincial governor said.

2008-01-28.title: Briton Opposed by Afghans Won’t Take U.N. Post
2008-01-28.abstract: The British politician Paddy Ashdown withdrew his name from consideration for the post of United Nations special envoy to Afghanistan, after being rejected by President Hamid Karzai.

2008-01-28.title: Russia Bars Opposition Candidate From March 2 Ballot
2008-01-28.abstract: The Central Election Commission  denied the registration of the opposition leader, former Prime Minister Mikhail M. Kasyanov, saying signatures he had collected in support of his candidacy were invalid.

2008-01-28.title: 19 Burned to Death in Violence in Kenya
2008-01-28.abstract: Ethnically driven violence intensified in Kenya on Sunday, and police officials said at least 19 people, including 11 children, were burned to death in a house by a mob.

2008-01-28.title: With a Whisper, Cuba’s Housing Market Booms
2008-01-28.abstract: Nearly all of Havana is owned by the state, but its residents are as obsessed with real estate as New Yorkers.

2008-01-28.title: 13 Arrested in Push to Stifle Turkish Ultranationalists Suspected in Political Killings
2008-01-28.abstract: In one of the biggest operations against Turkish ultranationalists in decades, authorities arrested 13 people including three retired military officers.

2008-01-29.title: Indonesia Buries Suharto
2008-01-29.abstract: Twenty-three hours after his death in Jakarta following a three-week hospitalization, Indonesia's former strongman, Suharto, was buried on Monday in a family mausoleum.

2008-01-29.title: Vengeance Reignites Kenyan City
2008-01-29.abstract: Kisumu, Kenya’s third largest city, erupted again on Monday as the police and military struggled to stop a wave of revenge killings.

2008-01-29.title: Europe Offers Serbia Deal to Sway Vote
2008-01-29.abstract: The E.U. signaled Serbia that its path to membership would be opened if it rejected a nationalist pro-Russian candidate in the presidential runoff next Sunday and apprehended war criminal suspects.

2008-01-29.title: Gorbachev, Rebuking Putin, Criticizes Russian Elections
2008-01-29.abstract: The remarks were the most vocal criticism to date by a prominent Russian political figure and followed the Russian government’s rejection of the only serious opposition candidate in the March 2 presidential election.

2008-01-29.title: Snowstorms in China Kill at Least 24
2008-01-29.abstract: Severe snowstorms over broad swaths of eastern and central China have wreaked havoc on traffic throughout the country.

2008-01-30.title: Kenyan Politician Assassinated
2008-01-30.abstract: A popular parliament member was gunned down outside his home in Nairobi, sparking clashes between mourners and police.

2008-01-30.title: Would-Be Peacemaker Killed in Kenya
2008-01-30.abstract: A freshman lawmaker who could have been one of the keys to unlocking Kenya’s crisis was shot dead.

2008-01-30.title: At White House, a Second Look at Iraq Troop Cuts
2008-01-30.abstract: President Bush is sending signals that the troop reductions in Iraq may not continue past this summer.

2008-01-30.title: White House Criticizes Envoy Over Iran
2008-01-30.abstract: White House officials expressed anger about how the United States ambassador to the United Nations sat beside the Iranian foreign minister at a panel of the World Economic Forum on Saturday.

2008-01-30.title: Britain: Guilty Plea in Plot to Behead Soldier
2008-01-30.abstract: A 37-year-old man from Birmingham, in the English midlands, has pleaded guilty to plotting to behead a British soldier. Parviz Khan, who described himself to investigators as a Muslim charity worker, was heard in covert recordings made by Britain’s security services outlining a plan to kidnap a soldier from Birmingham’s nightclub district and have him beheaded, the court in Leicester was told. Nigel Rumfitt, the prosecutor, said Mr. Khan’s plan was to videotape the killing and release the tape “to cause panic and fear within the British armed forces and wider public.” Three other men, all Muslims, have admitted offenses in the case, one of them a Gambian citizen tasked by Mr. Khan to identify a Muslim West African soldier serving in the British Army as the victim for the beheading plot, according to the prosecution. Mr. Khan was said to have shown the other men videos of terrorist beheadings in Iraq and Afghanistan.

2008-01-31.title: One Eye on the Fish, the Other on North Korea
2008-01-31.abstract: The  islands in the long-disputed waters near the North Korean coast present an especially tough challenge for relations between North and South Korea.

2008-01-31.title: Italian Leader Calls for Temporary Government
2008-01-31.abstract: Seeking to head off snap elections, Italy’s president, Giorgio Napolitano, asked the Senate president, Franco Marini, to try to form a temporary government and fix a flawed election law.

2008-01-31.title: Mutant Flu Virus Is Found That Resists Popular Drug
2008-01-31.abstract: The World Health Organization said that a small but significant percentage of the main influenza virus causing illness this winter is resistant to the anti-influenza drug Tamiflu.

2008-01-31.title: China Orders Relief Efforts, but Public Is Still Angry
2008-01-31.abstract: Prime Minister Wen Jiabao continued his tour of areas that have been heavily afflicted by the severe winter weather.

2008-01-31.title: Australia to Apologize to Aborigines for Past Mistreatment
2008-01-31.abstract: The new Australian government of Prime Minister Kevin Rudd will apologize for past mistreatment of the country’s Aboriginal minority.
